topics.title: Introduction to JavaScript
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what JavaScript is, where it runs, its role in the web stack alongside HTML and CSS, and the basics of the JavaScript engine and runtime.
topics.goal: Explain what JavaScript does, how it fits into a web page, and write a simple Hello World program.

topics.title: Setting Up Development Environment
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Install Node.js, a code editor, browser dev tools, and run your first JavaScript file. Understand the difference between running JS in the browser vs. Node.
topics.goal: Have a working JS development environment and run a simple script in both the browser console and Node.js.

topics.title: JavaScript Syntax and Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn JavaScript's core syntax: variables (var, let, const), primitive data types (string, number, boolean, null, undefined), type coercion, and comments.
topics.goal: Declare variables with let and const, identify primitive types, and understand type coercion pitfalls.

topics.title: Control Flow & Logic Building
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Write programs that make decisions with if/else, switch, ternary operators, and repeat work with for, while, and do-while loops. Understand truthy and falsy values.
topics.goal: Build a program that branches on conditions and iterates over a list using loops.

topics.title: Functions and Code Reusability
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Define and invoke functions, understand parameters, return values, scope (global vs. local), closures, and the difference between function declarations and arrow functions.
topics.goal: Write reusable functions, explain scope, and use arrow functions correctly.

topics.title: Debugging and Console Tools
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use browser DevTools and console methods (log, warn, error, table, time) to inspect variables, step through code with breakpoints, and read stack traces.
topics.goal: Set a breakpoint in DevTools, step through a buggy function, and identify the root cause of an error.

topics.title: JavaScript Arrays and Objects
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Store and manipulate collections with arrays (map, filter, reduce, find) and structured data with objects (properties, methods, destructuring, spread, and rest).
topics.goal: Transform an array of objects using map and filter, and destructure nested object properties.

topics.title: Working with DOM
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Select, create, modify, and remove HTML elements using the DOM API: querySelector, createElement, innerHTML, classList, and setAttribute.
topics.goal: Dynamically render a list of items into the page and update an element's content when a value changes.

topics.title: DOM Events and User Interaction
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Handle user interactions with addEventListener: click, input, submit, keyboard events. Understand event bubbling, capturing, event delegation, and preventDefault.
topics.goal: Build a form with validation using event listeners, and use event delegation on a dynamic list.

topics.title: Working with Real Data
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Consume REST APIs with the Fetch API and Axios: make GET and POST requests, handle JSON, deal with network errors, and manage loading and error states.
topics.goal: Fetch data from a public API, render it in the DOM, and handle both loading and error states.

topics.title: Storing Data in the Browser
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Persist data client-side with localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies. Understand their differences, size limits, security considerations, and when to use each.
topics.goal: Save and restore user preferences using localStorage, and explain when you would use sessionStorage instead.

topics.title: Modern JavaScript (ES6+) Essentials
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Adopt the modern JavaScript feature set: template literals, destructuring, spread/rest, optional chaining, nullish coalescing, Promises, async/await, and Symbol.
topics.goal: Refactor callback-based code to use async/await and use destructuring and optional chaining throughout.

topics.title: Organizing Code with Modules
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Split JavaScript into maintainable files using ES Modules (import/export). Understand named vs default exports, circular dependencies, and how bundlers resolve modules.
topics.goal: Refactor a single-file app into multiple modules with named and default exports.

topics.title: Basic Error Handling
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Handle synchronous errors with try/catch/finally, create custom Error subclasses, and catch async errors with .catch() and try/catch around await expressions.
topics.goal: Write a function that throws a custom error and handle it gracefully, including the async case.

topics.title: Basics of Package Management (npm/yarn)
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage project dependencies with npm or yarn: initialise a project, install/remove packages, understand package.json and package-lock.json, and run scripts.
topics.goal: Initialise a Node project, install a library, write a build script, and explain what node_modules contains.

topics.title: Collaborating with Git and GitHub
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Git and GitHub as a DevOps practitioner: branching strategies, pull request workflows, protected branches, and integrating version control into delivery pipelines.
topics.goal: Set up a Git branching strategy for a team project and open a pull request through GitHub Actions.

topics.title: Introducing Git and Version Control
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what Git is, why it's used, and how it differs from other version control systems.
topics.goal: Learner will understand the concept of version control and Git's role in software development.

topics.title: Installing Git and Basic Configuration
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Step-by-step installation of Git on various platforms and setting up user information.
topics.goal: Learner will be able to install Git and configure it with their name and email.

topics.title: Basics of Git Repositories
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to create new local Git repositories and clone remote ones.
topics.goal: Learner can create a Git repository and clone existing ones from platforms like GitHub.

topics.title: Basic Git Commands
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Hands-on with core commands for adding and saving changes in a Git repo.
topics.goal: Learner can track changes in a project and commit them, investigate project history and understand file-level changes. using Git.

topics.title: Branching and Merging
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to create and manage branches, and merge them back into the main branch.
topics.goal: Learner can use branches to work on features independently and safely merge them.

topics.title: Resolving Merge Conflicts
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand why merge conflicts occur and how to resolve them.
topics.goal: Learner can manually resolve merge conflicts and maintain code integrity.

topics.title: Working with Remote Repositories
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to interact with remote repositories on GitHub/GitLab.
topics.goal: Learner can sync local work with a remote repository using push and pull.

topics.title: Ignoring and Undoing Changes
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to prevent certain files from being tracked by Git and undo changes safely at different levels.
topics.goal: Learner can configure .gitignore to keep the repository clean and can roll back mistakes using appropriate Git commands.

topics.title: Rebasing and Interactive Rebase
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to rebase commits and rewrite history interactively.
topics.goal: Learner can clean up commit history and manage a linear project history.

topics.title: Stashing Work
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Temporarily save changes not ready to be committed and resume work later.
topics.goal: Learner can use git stash to manage interruptions without losing work.

topics.title: Tagging and Releases
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Git tags to mark release points and stable versions.
topics.goal: Learner can tag specific commits and manage versioning effectively.

topics.title: Git Hooks and Automation
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Git hooks to automate tasks before or after certain Git actions.
topics.goal: Learner can automate tasks like formatting or tests before commits/pushes.

topics.title: Advanced Git Troubleshooting
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Explore tools and techniques like reflog, cherry-pick, bisect, and reset strategies.
topics.goal: Learner can recover lost commits, isolate bugs, and confidently troubleshoot Git issues.

topics.title: Introduction to HTML
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what HTML is, how browsers parse and render it, the role of HTML in the web stack alongside CSS and JavaScript, and write your first HTML page.
topics.goal: Create a valid HTML page from scratch and explain what a browser does with it.

topics.title: HTML Document Structure
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the anatomy of an HTML document: DOCTYPE declaration, html, head, and body elements, charset, viewport meta tag, and how the document tree is structured.
topics.goal: Write a complete HTML document with correct doctype, charset, viewport, and title.

topics.title: Text and Formatting Elements
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Mark up text content using headings (h1–h6), paragraphs, emphasis (strong, em), line breaks, blockquotes, code elements, and understand inline vs. block formatting.
topics.goal: Structure an article with proper heading hierarchy, emphasised text, and a blockquote.

topics.title: Links and Navigation
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Create hyperlinks with the anchor tag: absolute vs. relative URLs, target attributes, linking to page sections with IDs, email links, and building accessible navigation menus.
topics.goal: Build a multi-page site with a nav bar and in-page jump links using anchor elements.

topics.title: Lists and Tables
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Organise content with ordered lists, unordered lists, definition lists, and tabular data with HTML tables: thead, tbody, tfoot, colspan, and rowspan.
topics.goal: Mark up a product comparison table with headers, body rows, and a totals row.

topics.title: Images and Multimedia
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Embed images with descriptive alt text, use figure and figcaption for captioned media, embed video and audio with the HTML5 media elements, and understand lazy loading.
topics.goal: Add a responsive image with alt text, a captioned figure, and an HTML5 video with controls.

topics.title: Forms and Input Elements
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build HTML forms: input types (text, email, password, checkbox, radio, file, range), textarea, select, label associations, fieldset, legend, and form submission with GET/POST.
topics.goal: Build a registration form with multiple input types, proper labels, and correct form attributes.

topics.title: Semantic HTML
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Replace generic divs with meaningful elements: header, nav, main, article, section, aside, footer, figure, time, and address — and understand why semantics matter for SEO and accessibility.
topics.goal: Refactor a div-heavy page to use semantic HTML5 elements throughout.

topics.title: Forms Validation and Attributes
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use HTML5 built-in validation: required, pattern, minlength, maxlength, min, max, type-based validation, and customise validation messages. Understand the constraint validation API.
topics.goal: Build a form that validates email, password strength, and a numeric range without JavaScript.

topics.title: Embedding Contents
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Embed external content with iframes, understand sandbox and allow attributes for security, embed maps and media players, and use the object and embed elements.
topics.goal: Safely embed a YouTube video and a Google Map using iframes with appropriate sandbox attributes.

topics.title: Accessibility in HTML
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Write HTML that works for everyone: ARIA roles and attributes, keyboard navigation, focus management, skip links, colour contrast requirements, and testing with a screen reader.
topics.goal: Audit a web page with a screen reader and fix missing ARIA labels, focus order, and skip navigation.

topics.title: Metadata and SEO Basics
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Optimise pages for search engines using meta description, Open Graph tags, Twitter cards, canonical links, robots meta, structured data (JSON-LD), and proper heading hierarchy.
topics.goal: Add correct meta tags, Open Graph properties, and JSON-LD structured data to a blog post page.

topics.title: CSS Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the three ways to apply CSS (inline, internal, external stylesheet), element, class, and ID selectors, how the cascade and inheritance resolve style conflicts, specificity calculation, and foundational properties (color, background, font-size, border, margin, padding).
topics.goal: Write valid CSS using selectors, apply styles via all three methods, and explain how specificity and the cascade determine which rule wins.

topics.title: Box Model & Positioning
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the CSS box model (content, padding, border, margin) and box-sizing: border-box, block vs inline vs inline-block display values, and the position property (static, relative, absolute, fixed, sticky) with z-index and stacking context.
topics.goal: Control element size and spacing using the box model, and position elements precisely using display and position properties.

topics.title: Flexbox
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the flex container and flex item model, the main and cross axes, justify-content, align-items, align-self, flex-wrap, and the flex shorthand (flex-grow, flex-shrink, flex-basis). Includes common Flexbox patterns: navigation bars, centered content, equal-height columns, and sticky footers.
topics.goal: Build one-dimensional layouts confidently using Flexbox — aligning, distributing, and reordering items without floats or hacks.

topics.title: Responsive Design
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers mobile-first design philosophy, media queries (@media with breakpoints for mobile, tablet, desktop), relative units (%, em, rem, vw, vh), responsive images (max-width: 100%), container queries (@container for component-level responsiveness), and common responsive layout patterns including card grids and holy-grail layouts.
topics.goal: Build layouts that adapt across all screen sizes using media queries, flexible units, and container queries for component-level responsiveness.

topics.title: Typography & Colors
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers loading custom fonts with @font-face and Google Fonts, font properties (font-family, size, weight, line-height, letter-spacing), color formats (hex, rgb, rgba, hsl, hsla), CSS custom properties (variables) for consistent theming, and color contrast for accessibility.
topics.goal: Style text professionally and manage colors consistently across a project using CSS custom properties.

topics.title: Animations & Transforms
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers CSS transitions (property, duration, timing-function, delay), 2D and 3D transforms (translate, rotate, scale, skew), and keyframe animations (@keyframes, animation-name, duration, timing-function, fill-mode, iteration-count). Includes performance considerations (transform vs position).
topics.goal: Add smooth transitions and keyframe animations to UI elements, and explain why transform-based animations are more performant.

topics.title: Advanced Selectors
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers CSS combinators (descendant, child >, adjacent +, general ~), attribute selectors, structural pseudo-classes (:nth-child, :first-of-type, :not), the modern relational pseudo-classes (:is(), :where(), :has()), pseudo-elements (::before, ::after, ::placeholder, ::selection), and specificity calculation across all selector types.
topics.goal: Target elements precisely with complex selectors and pseudo-classes — including :has() for parent selection — without adding extra HTML classes, and calculate specificity correctly.

topics.title: CSS Architecture
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers CSS naming methodologies (BEM, utility-first), CSS custom properties as design tokens, the @layer rule for explicit cascade management, native CSS nesting (no preprocessor needed), an overview of CSS Modules and scoped styles, and strategies for organising large stylesheets for long-term maintainability.
topics.goal: Write scalable, maintainable CSS using BEM naming, design tokens, cascade layers, and native nesting — with or without a preprocessor.

topics.title: Getting Started with Angular
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn what Angular is, why it is popular, and how modern web apps are built with it.

topics.title: Setting Up Your Angular Workspace
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Install Node.js, Angular CLI, and create your first Angular project with proper folder structure.

topics.title: Components and Templates
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn about Angular components, templates, and how they are the building blocks of applications

topics.title: Data Binding and Event Handling
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Explore property binding, event binding, interpolation, and two-way binding in Angular

topics.title: Directives and Conditional Rendering
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Work with built-in directives like *ngIf, *ngFor, ngClass, ngStyle, and learn to create custom directives.

topics.title: Services, Dependency Injection, and State Management
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to use services and Angular’s DI system to share logic and data across components

topics.title: Routing and Navigation
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Master Angular Router for single-page navigation, lazy loading, route guards, and child routes.

topics.title: Forms and User Input
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Work with template-driven and reactive forms to handle user inputs, validations, and submissions

topics.title: HTTP and API Integration
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Angular’s HttpClient to send requests, handle responses, and manage async data with Observables

topics.title: Pipes and Data Transformation
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Format and transform data using built-in pipes and learn how to create custom pipes.

topics.title: Angular Lifecycle and Change Detection
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand component lifecycle hooks, change detection, and how Angular updates the DOM

topics.title: Introduction to TypeScript
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers what TypeScript is and why it exists over plain JavaScript, installing TypeScript and the tsc compiler, running TypeScript with ts-node, the TypeScript Playground for quick experimentation, adding basic type annotations to variables and function parameters, the compilation step to JavaScript, and how TypeScript integrates with existing JavaScript codebases.
topics.goal: Explain what TypeScript adds over JavaScript, compile and run TypeScript files using tsc and ts-node, and add basic type annotations to variables and function signatures.

topics.title: TypeScript Configuration
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the tsconfig.json file and its key compilerOptions: target (ES version output), module (CommonJS, ESNext), lib, outDir, rootDir, and baseUrl. Deep-dives into strict mode and its constituent flags (strictNullChecks, noImplicitAny, strictFunctionTypes, strictPropertyInitialization). Also covers include/exclude path patterns, path aliases for clean imports, project references for monorepos, and the role of build tools like esbuild and swc alongside tsc.
topics.goal: Configure a TypeScript project with tsconfig.json, enable and understand each strict mode flag, and set up path aliases to eliminate relative import chains.

topics.title: Core Types
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers TypeScript's built-in primitive types (string, number, boolean, bigint, symbol), typed arrays and readonly arrays, tuples for fixed-length typed structures, numeric and string enums and const enums, the special types any, unknown, never, void, null, and undefined, union types (|) and type aliases with the type keyword, and the difference between type widening and type narrowing.
topics.goal: Annotate variables and function parameters with TypeScript's core types, choose between any and unknown correctly, define type aliases and basic union types, and explain the difference between null, undefined, void, and never.

topics.title: Functions & Classes
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers typing function parameters and return values, optional and default parameters, rest parameters with typed arrays, function overloads for multiple call signatures, the this parameter type annotation, TypeScript classes with explicit property declarations, access modifiers (public, private, protected) and readonly, the private class field syntax (#), abstract classes and abstract methods, implements for interface adherence, and static members and class expressions.
topics.goal: Write fully typed functions including overloads, implement TypeScript classes with access modifiers and abstract patterns, and enforce interface contracts on classes with implements.

topics.title: Advanced Type System
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers literal types and literal narrowing, discriminated union patterns (tagged unions) for exhaustive type-safe branching, type guards using typeof, instanceof, the in operator, and user-defined type predicates (is), conditional types (T extends U ? X : Y), the infer keyword inside conditional types, mapped types (transforming all properties of a type), template literal types (TypeScript 4.1+) for string-based type generation, and the satisfies operator (TypeScript 4.9+) for validating without widening.
topics.goal: Model complex domain data with discriminated unions, write type guard functions, create conditional and mapped types for type transformations, and use template literal types to generate string-based type variants.

topics.title: Modules & Declaration Files
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers ES Module syntax in TypeScript (import/export), module resolution strategies (node16, bundler), the purpose and structure of .d.ts declaration files, installing community type definitions via @types packages, writing your own ambient .d.ts files for untyped JavaScript libraries, module augmentation and declaration merging for extending existing library types, the declare keyword for ambient declarations, triple-slash reference directives, and why namespaces are considered legacy in modern TypeScript.
topics.goal: Manage imports and exports in a TypeScript project, install and interpret @types packages, write declaration files for untyped libraries, and use module augmentation to extend existing type definitions.

topics.title: Generics
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers the motivation for generics — writing reusable typed code without losing type information — generic function syntax with <T>, generic interfaces and generic classes, applying constraints with extends to limit accepted types, multiple type parameters and their interaction, default type parameters (TypeScript 4.7+), const type parameters (TypeScript 5.0+) for preserving literal types during inference, and common real-world generic patterns (wrapper types, factory functions, repository patterns).
topics.goal: Write generic functions and classes that are both reusable and type-safe, apply constraints with extends, use default type parameters, and understand how TypeScript infers type arguments automatically.

topics.title: Utility Types
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers TypeScript's complete set of built-in utility types: Partial<T>, Required<T>, Readonly<T>, Pick<T,K>, Omit<T,K>, Record<K,V>, Extract<T,U>, Exclude<T,U>, NonNullable<T>, ReturnType<F>, Parameters<F>, ConstructorParameters<T>, InstanceType<T>, and Awaited<T>. Covers how to chain and combine utility types for real-world DTO and API typing, and how to implement custom utility types from scratch using mapped types, conditional types, and the keyof and typeof operators.
topics.goal: Apply the standard TypeScript utility types to derive and transform types for DTOs, API responses, and component props, and build custom utility types using mapped and conditional types.

topics.title: Decorators & Framework Integration
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers TypeScript decorators using the stable Stage 3 syntax (no experimental flags required since TypeScript 5.0), class decorators, method decorators, property and accessor decorators, parameter decorators, decorator factories, and the TC39 Decorator Metadata proposal (stable since TypeScript 5.2). Shows how Angular uses @Component, @Injectable, @Pipe, and @Directive, how NestJS uses @Controller, @Get, @Injectable, and validation decorators, and how to type React component props, hooks, context, and event handlers correctly in TypeScript.
topics.goal: Write and compose class and method decorators using Stage 3 syntax, explain how Angular and NestJS decorators work under the hood via metadata, and correctly type Angular services, components, and React component props with TypeScript.

topics.title: Interfaces & Type Aliases
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers defining object contracts with interface, optional (?) and readonly properties, extending interfaces with extends and merging via declaration merging, intersection types (&) for type composition, the key differences between interface and type alias (primitives, declaration merging, extending), structural (duck) typing and what it means for assignability, index signatures for dynamic keys, and practical guidance on when to prefer interface versus type.
topics.goal: Define object shapes using both interface and type alias, understand structural typing and assignability, compose types with extends and intersections, and choose the correct tool for each use case.

topics.title: Java Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn Java's syntax from scratch: variables, primitive and reference types, operators, control flow (if, switch, for, while), and how to compile and run a Java program with the JDK.
topics.goal: Write, compile, and run a Java program that uses variables, loops, and conditionals to solve a simple problem.

topics.title: Methods & OOP
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Define and call methods with parameters and return types. Introduce object-oriented programming: classes, objects, constructors, access modifiers, encapsulation, and the 'this' keyword.
topics.goal: Model a real-world entity as a Java class with fields, a constructor, getters/setters, and a business method.

topics.title: Core Java APIs
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use the Java Collections Framework: ArrayList, LinkedList, HashMap, HashSet, TreeMap. Work with Strings (StringBuilder, String methods), the Math class, and java.time for date/time handling.
topics.goal: Write a program that reads a list of words, counts their frequency in a HashMap, and prints them sorted.

topics.title: Exception Handling
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Handle runtime errors with try/catch/finally, understand checked vs. unchecked exceptions, create custom exception classes, and use multi-catch and try-with-resources.
topics.goal: Write a method that reads a file, handles checked exceptions properly, and uses try-with-resources for cleanup.

topics.title: Advanced OOP
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Go deeper into OOP: inheritance, method overriding, abstract classes, interfaces (with default methods), polymorphism, and common design patterns — Strategy, Factory, and Singleton.
topics.goal: Implement a plugin system using interfaces and polymorphism and apply the Strategy pattern to swap algorithms.

topics.title: Multithreading
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Run code concurrently with Thread, Runnable, and ExecutorService. Handle shared state with synchronised blocks, locks, and concurrent collections. Understand deadlocks, race conditions, and volatile.
topics.goal: Build a thread-safe task queue using ExecutorService and demonstrate a data race, then fix it with synchronisation.

topics.title: File I/O & Streams
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Read and write files with java.io (FileReader, BufferedWriter) and java.nio.file (Files, Path). Understand byte streams vs. character streams and the NIO2 API for modern file operations.
topics.goal: Write a program that reads a CSV file with NIO2, processes each row, and writes the output to a new file.

topics.title: Generics & Advanced Collections
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Write type-safe code with generics (bounded wildcards, generic methods, generic classes). Use the Stream API for functional-style operations: filter, map, reduce, collect, and flatMap.
topics.goal: Write a generic repository class and use Stream API to filter, transform, and aggregate a collection.

topics.title: Java Memory & JVM
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand the JVM's memory model: heap, stack, metaspace, and the garbage collector. Learn to diagnose memory leaks, tune GC settings, interpret heap dumps, and use profiling tools.
topics.goal: Profile a Java application to find a memory leak, fix it, and explain what the GC was collecting before and after.

topics.title: Java Ecosystem
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Navigate the Java ecosystem: Maven and Gradle for build and dependency management, JUnit 5 for testing, Lombok for boilerplate reduction, and an introduction to the Spring framework.
topics.goal: Create a Maven project, add dependencies, write JUnit 5 tests, and use Lombok to simplify a model class.

topics.title: Spring Boot Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Bootstrap a Spring Boot application with Spring Initializr. Understand auto-configuration, application.properties/yml, starter dependencies, and the opinionated Spring Boot project structure.
topics.goal: Create a Spring Boot app with Initializr, add a starter, and run it with a custom application property.

topics.title: REST APIs
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build RESTful APIs with Spring MVC: @RestController, @GetMapping, @PostMapping, @PutMapping, @DeleteMapping, @PathVariable, @RequestParam, @RequestBody, and ResponseEntity.
topics.goal: Build a full CRUD REST API for a resource with proper HTTP status codes and request/response DTOs.

topics.title: Dependency Injection & Beans
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the Spring IoC container: @Component, @Service, @Repository, @Controller stereotypes, @Bean in @Configuration classes, @Autowired, constructor injection, and bean scopes.
topics.goal: Build a layered service using constructor injection and explain why it is preferred over field injection.

topics.title: Data Access
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Connect to a relational database with Spring Data JPA: entity mapping with @Entity, repositories (CrudRepository, JpaRepository), JPQL and native queries, pagination, and transaction management with @Transactional.
topics.goal: Build a JPA entity, a JpaRepository with a custom query method, and a paginated endpoint backed by the database.

topics.title: Validation & Exception Handling
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Validate request bodies with Bean Validation annotations (@NotNull, @Size, @Email, @Valid). Handle exceptions globally with @ControllerAdvice and @ExceptionHandler for clean error responses.
topics.goal: Add request validation to a REST endpoint and write a global exception handler that returns standardised error payloads.

topics.title: Security
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Secure Spring Boot applications with Spring Security: authentication vs. authorisation, HTTP Basic, JWT-based stateless auth, role-based access control with @PreAuthorize, and CORS configuration.
topics.goal: Add JWT authentication to a REST API so protected endpoints return 401 for unauthenticated requests.

topics.title: Spring Boot Advanced Features
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Use Spring Boot's production-ready features: Actuator endpoints for health and metrics, custom health indicators, Profiles for environment separation, caching with @Cacheable, async with @Async, and reactive with WebFlux.
topics.goal: Enable Actuator, expose a custom health indicator, add method-level caching, and run an async task with @Async.

topics.title: Testing
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Test Spring Boot applications: unit tests with JUnit 5 and Mockito, @WebMvcTest for controller slice tests, @DataJpaTest for repository tests, @SpringBootTest for full integration tests, and TestContainers.
topics.goal: Write a @WebMvcTest for a controller and a @DataJpaTest for a repository, using MockMvc and an in-memory DB.

topics.title: Microservices
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Apply microservice patterns with Spring Boot: inter-service communication (RestTemplate, WebClient, Feign), service discovery (Eureka), API gateway, circuit breaker (Resilience4j), and distributed tracing.
topics.goal: Build two Spring Boot services that communicate via a Feign client with a circuit breaker for fault tolerance.

topics.title: Deployment of Java Applications
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Package and deploy Spring Boot applications: build a Docker image with a multi-stage Dockerfile, deploy to Kubernetes, configure environment-specific properties, and use GitHub Actions for CI/CD.
topics.goal: Containerise a Spring Boot app, push it to a registry, and deploy it to a Kubernetes cluster using a Helm chart.

topics.title: Node.js Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand how Node.js works: the V8 engine, the event loop, single-threaded non-blocking I/O, the REPL, running scripts, and how Node differs from browser JavaScript.
topics.goal: Run a Node.js script from the terminal and explain what makes Node's I/O model non-blocking.

topics.title: Asynchronous Programming
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Master async patterns in Node.js: callbacks and callback hell, Promises and chaining, async/await, Promise.all, and error handling in async code.
topics.goal: Refactor a nested callback chain to async/await with proper try/catch error handling.

topics.title: Node.js Core Modules
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Node's built-in module system: require vs. import, the module cache, and the most-used core modules — path, os, url, util, and events.
topics.goal: Write a script that uses path, os, and events to log machine info and emit a custom event.

topics.title: Events & Streams
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use Node's EventEmitter to build event-driven systems. Understand readable, writable, transform, and duplex streams — and how piping streams avoids memory issues with large data.
topics.goal: Build a Transform stream that compresses data on the fly and pipe it from a file read to a file write.

topics.title: File System & Networking
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Read, write, and watch files with the fs module (sync vs async vs streaming). Build a basic HTTP server with the http module and understand request/response objects.
topics.goal: Write a Node HTTP server that reads a JSON file and responds with its contents as an API response.

topics.title: Process & Child Processes
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use the process object (env, argv, exit, signals) and spawn child processes with child_process: spawn, exec, execFile, and fork for CPU-intensive work.
topics.goal: Spawn a child process to run a shell command and stream its output back to the parent process.

topics.title: Debugging & Testing
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Debug Node apps with the built-in debugger and VS Code's Node.js debugger. Write tests with Jest or Vitest: unit tests for pure functions, and integration tests for HTTP endpoints using Supertest.
topics.goal: Set a breakpoint in a running Node server via VS Code, and write an integration test for a REST endpoint.

topics.title: Performance & Scaling
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Profile Node.js apps to find CPU and memory bottlenecks. Use the cluster module and worker_threads for parallel execution. Understand the limits of the event loop and how to avoid blocking it.
topics.goal: Profile a CPU-intensive endpoint, offload it to a worker thread, and verify improved response time.

topics.title: Package Management & Deployment
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage a Node project professionally: package.json scripts, semantic versioning, lock files, private vs. published packages, environment variables with dotenv, and deploying to a VPS or PaaS.
topics.goal: Configure a Node app with npm scripts for dev/build/start, manage secrets via dotenv, and deploy to Railway or Render.

topics.title: Introduction to React
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what React is, why it exists, the virtual DOM concept, component-based architecture, and how React fits into the modern frontend ecosystem alongside bundlers and npm.
topics.goal: Explain the virtual DOM, describe what a component is, and identify where React fits in a web stack.

topics.title: Environment Setup
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Bootstrap a React project with Vite or Create React App, understand the project structure, run the development server, and make your first code change with hot reload.
topics.goal: Scaffold a React project with Vite, run it in the browser, and explain the role of each generated file.

topics.title: JSX & Rendering Elements
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Write JSX — React's HTML-like syntax compiled by Babel. Understand expressions in JSX, self-closing tags, fragments, and how JSX maps to React.createElement calls.
topics.goal: Write a JSX component that renders a dynamic list and includes conditional output using ternary expressions.

topics.title: Components and Props
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build functional components, pass data via props, use destructuring, set default props, understand prop types, and grasp component composition and the children prop.
topics.goal: Build a reusable Card component that accepts title, body, and children props from a parent.

topics.title: State and Lifecycle
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Manage local component state with useState, understand how re-renders are triggered, use useEffect for side effects and cleanup, and model the component lifecycle with hooks.
topics.goal: Build a counter with useState and a data-fetching component that cleans up its subscription with useEffect.

topics.title: Event Handling and Conditional Rendering
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Handle DOM events in React: onClick, onChange, onSubmit. Render content conditionally with ternary operators, logical &&, and switch statements inside JSX.
topics.goal: Build a toggle component that shows/hides content and a form that disables submit until fields are valid.

topics.title: Lists and Keys
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Render dynamic lists by mapping arrays to JSX elements, understand why React requires unique key props, and learn the consequences of using array index as a key.
topics.goal: Render a filterable list of items using Array.map() with correct unique keys.

topics.title: Forms and Controlled Components
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Manage form state with controlled components: bind input values to state, handle onChange, build multi-field forms, and understand uncontrolled components and refs as an alternative.
topics.goal: Build a login form as a fully controlled component and handle submission with validation.

topics.title: React Hooks Deep Dive
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Master the complete hooks API: useReducer for complex state, useRef for DOM access and persistent values, useMemo and useCallback for performance, and the rules of hooks.
topics.goal: Refactor a multi-state component to useReducer and optimise a child re-render with useCallback.

topics.title: Context API and State Management
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Share state across a component tree with useContext and createContext. Understand when Context solves the problem vs. when a dedicated state library (Zustand, Redux) is warranted.
topics.goal: Build a theme switcher that provides theme state to deeply nested components via Context.

topics.title: Routing and Navigation
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Add multi-page navigation to a React SPA with React Router v6: Route, Link, useNavigate, useParams, nested routes, protected routes, and the Outlet component.
topics.goal: Build a multi-page app with nested routes, a protected dashboard route, and URL parameter-driven content.

topics.title: React with APIs
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Integrate REST APIs with fetch, Axios, and React Query (TanStack Query): manage loading, error, and success states, cache responses, and handle paginated data.
topics.goal: Fetch and display paginated data from a public API using React Query with loading and error states.

topics.title: Performance Optimization
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Diagnose and fix React performance problems: React.memo, useMemo, useCallback, code splitting with React.lazy and Suspense, and profiling with React DevTools.
topics.goal: Profile a slow component tree, identify unnecessary re-renders, and fix them with memo and useMemo.

topics.title: Custom Hooks
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Extract and share stateful logic across components using custom hooks. Build hooks for data fetching, window resize, debouncing, and local storage persistence.
topics.goal: Build a useFetch custom hook that abstracts GET request logic and returns loading, data, and error state.

topics.title: Testing React Applications
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Test React components with React Testing Library and Jest: render, query by role, fire events, test async behaviour, mock API calls with MSW, and write integration-style tests.
topics.goal: Write tests for a form component that cover validation, submission, and a mocked API response.

topics.title: Next.js and Server-Side Rendering
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Extend React with Next.js: pages vs. App Router, SSR with getServerSideProps and Server Components, SSG, ISR, API routes, and deployment to Vercel.
topics.goal: Build a blog with Next.js using static generation for posts and server-side rendering for a dynamic page.

topics.title: React Design Patterns & Architecture
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Apply proven React architecture patterns: compound components, render props, higher-order components, container/presenter split, feature-sliced design, and error boundaries.
topics.goal: Refactor a monolithic component using the compound component pattern and add an Error Boundary.

topics.title: Deployment and Best Practices
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Ship React apps to production: optimise the build (tree-shaking, chunk splitting, asset hashing), configure environment variables, add CI/CD, and deploy to Vercel, Netlify, or a custom server.
topics.goal: Deploy a React app to Vercel with environment variables and a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that runs tests before deploy.

topics.title: Nx - What, Why, When?
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: What is Nx?

topics.title: Features of Nx
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: What Nx actually does?

topics.title: Introduction to Tailwind CSS
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what Tailwind CSS is and why the utility-first approach is a paradigm shift from traditional CSS. Covers the difference between utility classes and component-based CSS frameworks like Bootstrap, and why Tailwind produces smaller, more maintainable stylesheets.
topics.goal: Understand the utility-first philosophy and how Tailwind differs from traditional CSS frameworks.

topics.title: Setup & Configuration
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Set up Tailwind CSS in a project from scratch. Covers installation via npm, the tailwind.config.js file, PostCSS integration, content path configuration, and the three core directives (@tailwind base, components, utilities).
topics.goal: Install and configure Tailwind CSS in a new or existing project, understanding what each config option does.

topics.title: Core Utility Classes
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Master the essential Tailwind utility classes: background and text colors, margin and padding, width and height, border, opacity, and display utilities. Learn how the default scale system works and how to read the documentation effectively.
topics.goal: Apply core utility classes confidently to style any basic HTML element.

topics.title: Flexbox & Grid Utilities
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build modern layouts using Tailwind's Flexbox and CSS Grid utility classes. Covers flex-direction, justify, align, gap, grid-cols, col-span, and common real-world layout patterns like navbars, card grids, and sidebars.
topics.goal: Build complete page layouts using only Tailwind flex and grid utilities.

topics.title: Responsive Design
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use Tailwind's mobile-first responsive prefixes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:, 2xl:) to build layouts that adapt to any screen size. Covers how breakpoints work, the mobile-first approach, and practical patterns for responsive navigation, grids, and typography.
topics.goal: Build fully responsive layouts using Tailwind's breakpoint prefix system.

topics.title: State & Pseudo-class Variants
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Style interactive states using Tailwind's variant prefixes: hover:, focus:, active:, disabled:, visited:, and the powerful group- and peer- patterns for parent/sibling-driven state changes. Covers building interactive buttons, form inputs, and dropdown menus.
topics.goal: Apply state variants to create interactive, accessible UI components without writing custom CSS.

topics.title: Typography & Color System
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Work with Tailwind's complete typography utilities — font size, weight, line height, letter spacing, text transform, and decoration — alongside the full 9-shade color palette system. Learn to use the @tailwindcss/typography plugin for prose content.
topics.goal: Build consistent, design-system-quality typography and color usage across an application.

topics.title: Spacing, Sizing & Layout Utilities
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Deep dive into Tailwind's spacing scale — margin, padding, space-between, gap — and sizing utilities including min/max width and height, aspect ratio, overflow control, and the container class. Understand how the 4px base unit creates a consistent visual rhythm.
topics.goal: Apply precise spacing and sizing to produce pixel-perfect layouts using the Tailwind spacing scale.

topics.title: Component Patterns with @apply
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use the @apply directive to extract repeated utility combinations into reusable CSS component classes. Covers when to use @apply vs. framework components (React/Angular/Vue), building a button system, form input styles, and badge components.
topics.goal: Identify when to extract utilities into components using @apply and build a consistent component library.

topics.title: Dark Mode
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Implement dark mode using Tailwind's dark: variant. Covers the difference between media strategy (prefers-color-scheme) and class strategy (manual toggle), toggling with JavaScript, and building a complete UI that looks great in both light and dark themes.
topics.goal: Build a complete light/dark theme switch using Tailwind's dark: variant.

topics.title: Custom Theme Configuration
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Extend and override Tailwind's default theme in tailwind.config.js. Covers adding custom colors, fonts, spacing values, border radii, and breakpoints. Understand the difference between extend (adds to defaults) and override (replaces defaults), and how to use CSS variables as theme values.
topics.goal: Configure a fully custom design system in tailwind.config.js that matches a brand style guide.

topics.title: Animations & Transitions
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Add motion to UIs using Tailwind's transition, duration, ease, and animate utilities. Covers the four built-in animations (spin, ping, pulse, bounce), adding custom keyframe animations via config, and accessibility best practices with prefers-reduced-motion.
topics.goal: Build smooth, accessible animations and transitions using Tailwind utilities and custom keyframes.

topics.title: Tailwind Plugins & Ecosystem
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Extend Tailwind with official and community plugins. Covers @tailwindcss/typography (beautiful prose), @tailwindcss/forms (consistent form resets), @tailwindcss/aspect-ratio, and writing a simple custom plugin. Also covers the Headless UI and Radix UI ecosystem.
topics.goal: Extend Tailwind with plugins and integrate it with the wider headless component ecosystem.

topics.title: Production Optimization
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Ship Tailwind-based applications with minimal CSS bundle size. Covers how the JIT engine works, content path configuration for tree-shaking, safelist patterns for dynamic class names, and analysing output CSS size. Compares development vs production build output.
topics.goal: Configure Tailwind for production builds that are optimally small and correctly tree-shaken.

topics.title: What is a Computer?
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand what a computer is and how it works at a basic level. Covers the difference between hardware and software, the main types of computers (desktop, laptop, tablet, server), and how they are used in everyday life and work.
topics.goal: Explain what a computer is, distinguish hardware from software, and identify the main types of computers.

topics.title: Hardware Components
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the physical components that make up a computer — the CPU (processor), RAM (memory), storage (HDD/SSD), motherboard, and input/output devices like monitors, keyboards, and mice. Understand what each component does and how they work together.
topics.goal: Identify the main hardware components of a computer and describe the role of each.

topics.title: Keyboard & Mouse Skills
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build confidence using the keyboard and mouse — key positions, typing basics, essential keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Ctrl+Z, Alt+Tab), and mouse gestures including click, double-click, right-click, scroll, and drag-and-drop.
topics.goal: Use a keyboard and mouse competently for everyday computer tasks and common keyboard shortcuts.

topics.title: Operating System Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the role of the operating system and get comfortable with the Windows environment — the desktop, taskbar, Start menu, system tray, and notification area. Learn to open, close, resize, and switch between application windows.
topics.goal: Navigate the Windows operating system confidently, managing windows and accessing key system features.

topics.title: File & Folder Management
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Master the skills of organising files and folders — creating and naming folders, moving and copying files, using cut/copy/paste, deleting files and using the Recycle Bin, and searching for files. Learn good file naming conventions and folder structure habits.
topics.goal: Organise files and folders in Windows Explorer using create, move, copy, rename, and delete operations.

topics.title: Storage & Memory
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand different types of storage — internal hard drives (HDD and SSD), USB flash drives, memory cards, and cloud storage. Learn about file sizes (KB, MB, GB, TB), how to check available storage space, and how to safely eject removable devices.
topics.goal: Understand storage types, file sizes, and manage storage devices safely.

topics.title: Working with Applications
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to install and uninstall software, open and close applications, manage multiple open windows, and use the taskbar to switch between programs. Covers finding installed apps, using the Windows search bar, and basic app settings.
topics.goal: Install, manage, and work with multiple applications in Windows confidently.

topics.title: Printing & Peripherals
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Set up and use peripheral devices — connecting a printer, sending a document to print, managing the print queue, scanning a document, and connecting other external devices (USB, Bluetooth). Covers basic troubleshooting when a device is not detected.
topics.goal: Set up and use printers and common peripherals, and manage print jobs.

topics.title: Basic Troubleshooting
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn how to handle everyday computer problems — what to do when a program freezes, how to use Task Manager to end unresponsive applications, why restarting fixes many issues, how to check for and install Windows updates, and how to read basic error messages.
topics.goal: Diagnose and resolve common everyday computer problems independently.

topics.title: Network & Internet Basics
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Understand how computers connect to the internet — WiFi vs wired connections, how to connect to a network, what an IP address is, the difference between a LAN and the internet, and basic network troubleshooting like checking your connection status.
topics.goal: Connect a computer to a network and understand basic networking concepts.

topics.title: Computer Security Basics
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn the fundamentals of keeping your computer secure — creating strong passwords, understanding why antivirus software matters, recognising suspicious programs, keeping software updated to patch security vulnerabilities, and basic safe computing habits.
topics.goal: Apply basic computer security practices to protect your system and data.

topics.title: Customising Your Computer
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Personalise and configure your computer for comfort and efficiency — adjusting display settings (brightness, resolution, text size), setting up accessibility features, creating desktop shortcuts, managing startup programs, and basic system settings.
topics.goal: Configure Windows settings for personal preferences and productivity.

topics.title: Web Browsers & Browsing
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn to navigate the internet using a web browser. Covers the browser interface (address bar, tabs, navigation buttons), opening and managing multiple tabs, bookmarking favourite sites, clearing history and cache, downloading files, and setting a homepage.
topics.goal: Use a web browser confidently to navigate websites, manage tabs, and download files.

topics.title: Search Engines & Research
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use search engines effectively to find reliable information. Covers Google search techniques (keywords, phrases, filters), evaluating whether a source is trustworthy, understanding the difference between search results and advertisements, and using Google Images and Maps.
topics.goal: Search the internet effectively and critically evaluate the reliability of online information.

topics.title: Email Fundamentals
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Master professional email communication. Covers creating an email account (Gmail/Outlook), composing and replying to emails, using CC and BCC correctly, attaching files and images, managing inbox folders, spam filters, and basic email etiquette for a professional setting.
topics.goal: Compose, send, receive, and organise emails professionally using Gmail or Outlook.

topics.title: Cloud Storage & File Sharing
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Use cloud storage services to save, access, and share files from anywhere. Covers Google Drive and OneDrive — uploading files, creating folders, sharing with view/edit permissions, generating share links, and syncing files between your computer and the cloud.
topics.goal: Upload, organise, and share files using cloud storage services like Google Drive or OneDrive.

topics.title: Video Conferencing
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Participate in and host online meetings professionally. Covers joining and creating meetings in Zoom and Microsoft Teams, managing audio and video settings, using the chat panel, screen sharing, muting/unmuting, and meeting etiquette for professional environments.
topics.goal: Join and host video calls on Zoom and Microsoft Teams and use key meeting features professionally.

topics.title: Online Communication Tools
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Communicate effectively using digital messaging tools. Covers WhatsApp Web for personal and informal business use, Slack basics for team communication (channels, direct messages, mentions), professional messaging etiquette, and understanding the right tool for the right type of communication.
topics.goal: Use messaging and collaboration tools appropriately and professionally in a workplace context.

topics.title: Digital Safety & Privacy
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Stay safe in the digital world. Covers creating and managing strong passwords, setting up two-factor authentication (2FA), recognising phishing emails and scam websites, understanding data privacy settings on social media and apps, and protecting personal information online.
topics.goal: Identify digital threats, protect personal accounts with strong passwords and 2FA, and avoid common online scams.

topics.title: Social Media & Professional Networking
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Build a professional online presence. Covers setting up and optimising a LinkedIn profile, understanding the difference between personal and professional social media use, connecting with colleagues and peers, and the basics of professional digital communication and etiquette.
topics.goal: Create a professional LinkedIn profile and understand appropriate professional online behaviour.

topics.title: Online Forms & Digital Documents
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Handle digital documents and forms professionally. Covers filling out online government and workplace forms, signing documents electronically, working with PDF files (view, annotate, fill forms), converting between file formats (Word to PDF), and submitting documents through online portals.
topics.goal: Complete online forms, handle PDF documents, and submit digital documents through online systems.

topics.title: Digital Productivity Tools
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use digital tools to stay organised and productive. Covers Google Calendar and Outlook Calendar for scheduling, Google Keep and OneNote for note-taking, task management with Google Tasks or Microsoft To-Do, and an introduction to Google Workspace and Microsoft 365 online apps for collaborative working.
topics.goal: Use digital calendar, notes, and task tools to manage time and stay organised at work.

topics.title: Introduction to Microsoft Word
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Get started with Microsoft Word. Covers the ribbon interface, creating a new document, opening and saving files, understanding file formats (.docx, .pdf), the Quick Access Toolbar, and zoom and view options. By the end you will be comfortable creating and saving your first Word document.
topics.goal: Navigate the Word interface, create a new document, and save it in different formats.

topics.title: Text Formatting & Styles
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Format text professionally in Word. Covers font selection, size, bold, italic, underline, text colour, highlighting, text alignment (left, centre, right, justified), line spacing, and applying Heading styles for document structure. Understand when to use direct formatting vs styles.
topics.goal: Format text and apply heading styles to produce well-structured, professional-looking documents.

topics.title: Paragraphs, Lists & Tables
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Structure document content with lists and tables. Covers creating bulleted and numbered lists, adjusting list indentation, inserting and formatting tables (add/remove rows and columns, merge cells, apply table styles), and using paragraph spacing for clean layouts.
topics.goal: Create structured documents using lists and tables to organise information clearly.

topics.title: Page Layout & Headers/Footers
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Control the layout of your Word documents. Covers setting margins, page orientation (portrait/landscape), paper size (A4), inserting page breaks, adding headers and footers with document title and page numbers, and using the ruler for indentation.
topics.goal: Configure page layout settings and add professional headers, footers, and page numbers.

topics.title: Saving, Printing & Sharing Word Documents
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Finish and distribute Word documents professionally. Covers Save vs Save As, exporting to PDF, configuring print settings (page range, copies, printer selection), using print preview, sharing documents via email and OneDrive, and setting document permissions.
topics.goal: Save documents in multiple formats, configure print settings, and share documents with others.

topics.title: Introduction to Microsoft Excel
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Get comfortable with Excel. Covers the workbook and worksheet structure, cells, rows, columns, and cell references (A1 notation), navigating with keyboard shortcuts, entering text and numbers, and saving Excel files. Understand what Excel is used for and when to use it.
topics.goal: Navigate the Excel interface, understand the workbook structure, and enter basic data into cells.

topics.title: Data Entry & Cell Formatting
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Enter and format data in Excel professionally. Covers entering different data types (text, numbers, dates), formatting cells for currency, percentages, and dates, adjusting column widths and row heights, applying cell borders and fill colours, and using the Format Cells dialog.
topics.goal: Enter data correctly and apply appropriate cell formatting to make spreadsheets readable.

topics.title: Basic Formulas & Functions
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Calculate with Excel using formulas and functions. Covers writing basic formulas with +, -, *, /, and using the essential functions: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MIN, and MAX. Understand cell references in formulas, AutoFill to copy formulas across ranges, and using the AutoSum button.
topics.goal: Write basic formulas and use SUM, AVERAGE, COUNT, MIN, and MAX functions to calculate data.

topics.title: Charts & Graphs
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Visualise data with Excel charts. Covers selecting data and inserting a chart, choosing the right chart type (bar, column, pie, line), adding a chart title and labels, formatting chart colours, and moving a chart to a new sheet or embedding it in the worksheet.
topics.goal: Create, format, and interpret bar, column, pie, and line charts from spreadsheet data.

topics.title: VLOOKUP & IF Functions
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Work with two of Excel's most valuable functions. VLOOKUP: look up data from another table or range using a key value. IF: apply conditional logic to return different results based on a condition. Covers nested IF statements, common VLOOKUP errors, and combining both functions in practical scenarios.
topics.goal: Use VLOOKUP to look up data across ranges and IF to apply conditional logic in spreadsheets.

topics.title: Sorting, Filtering & Data Validation
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage and control data quality in Excel. Covers sorting data alphabetically and numerically (single and multi-level), using AutoFilter to show only specific rows, creating custom filter criteria, and setting up Data Validation rules to restrict cell input (dropdown lists, number ranges, date limits).
topics.goal: Sort and filter datasets effectively and use data validation to maintain data integrity.

topics.title: Pivot Tables
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Summarise and analyse large datasets with Pivot Tables. Covers inserting a Pivot Table, choosing rows, columns, values, and filters, changing the value calculation (sum, count, average), refreshing data, and creating a basic Pivot Chart. Understand when a Pivot Table is the right tool.
topics.goal: Create a Pivot Table to summarise and analyse data by different categories.

topics.title: Introduction to Microsoft PowerPoint
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Get started with PowerPoint. Covers the PowerPoint interface, creating a new presentation, adding and deleting slides, choosing slide layouts, entering text in placeholders, inserting images, and saving your presentation. Understand how PowerPoint is used in professional settings.
topics.goal: Create a basic PowerPoint presentation with multiple slides, layouts, text, and images.

topics.title: Slide Design & Themes
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Design visually consistent presentations. Covers applying a theme, customising theme colours and fonts, using the Slide Master to make global changes, inserting shapes and SmartArt, aligning objects on a slide, and basic design principles for readable, professional-looking slides.
topics.goal: Apply themes, customise slide design, and use the Slide Master for consistent presentation styling.

topics.title: Animations & Transitions
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Add motion to your presentations. Covers applying slide transitions (fade, push, wipe), adding entrance and exit animations to text and objects, controlling animation timing and order, and using the Animation Pane to fine-tune effects. Learn when animations add value vs. when they distract.
topics.goal: Apply and configure slide transitions and object animations professionally.

topics.title: Presenting & Sharing
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Deliver and share your presentation confidently. Covers starting and navigating a slideshow (keyboard shortcuts, laser pointer), using Presenter View (see notes, see next slide), setting up on a second monitor, exporting as PDF, sharing via email or OneDrive, and packaging a presentation for offline use.
topics.goal: Deliver a slideshow using Presenter View and export or share a presentation in multiple formats.

topics.title: Keyboard Skills & Touch Typing
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Build a proper typing foundation. Covers correct posture and hand positioning, home row keys (ASDF and JKL;), touch typing technique (typing without looking at the keyboard), understanding the full keyboard layout, and beginning to build typing speed with accuracy.
topics.goal: Demonstrate correct touch typing technique on the home row and work toward consistent speed and accuracy.

topics.title: Data Entry Best Practices
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the professional standards for data entry work. Covers the priority of accuracy over speed, consistent formatting conventions (dates, phone numbers, names), understanding source documents, double-checking entries, and the consequences of incorrect data in business systems.
topics.goal: Apply professional data entry standards — consistent formatting, accuracy-first approach, and source document verification.

topics.title: Working with Forms & Templates
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Work efficiently with pre-built forms and document templates. Covers using Word templates to produce consistent documents (invoices, letters, reports), filling in form fields in PDF and Word documents, understanding required vs optional fields, and saving completed forms with appropriate naming conventions.
topics.goal: Use Word and PDF templates to produce standardised, professional documents consistently.

topics.title: Keyboard Shortcuts Mastery
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Dramatically increase productivity with keyboard shortcuts. Covers essential Windows shortcuts (Win+D, Alt+Tab, Win+L), universal shortcuts (Ctrl+C/X/V/Z/Y/S/P/F), Office-specific shortcuts (Ctrl+B/I/U in Word, Ctrl+Home/End in Excel), and navigation shortcuts for working without the mouse.
topics.goal: Use the most common Windows and Microsoft Office keyboard shortcuts fluently to work faster.

topics.title: Copy-Paste & Data Transfer
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Move data between applications efficiently and correctly. Covers clipboard operations, Paste Special (paste values only, paste formatting), transferring data between Excel and Word, copying from web pages to documents while preserving or removing formatting, and using Find & Replace to clean copied data.
topics.goal: Transfer data cleanly between applications using copy-paste, Paste Special, and Find & Replace.

topics.title: Spreadsheet Data Management
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage large sets of data in Excel efficiently. Covers working with large spreadsheets (freeze panes, split views), using Ctrl+End to navigate, creating structured data tables, entering data consistently across large ranges, and using Excel's Table feature for automatic formatting and formula extension.
topics.goal: Navigate and manage large Excel datasets efficiently using freeze panes, tables, and structured ranges.

topics.title: Data Accuracy & Validation
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Ensure data is accurate, consistent, and clean. Covers proofreading techniques, using Find & Replace to correct repeated errors, removing duplicate entries, trimming extra spaces with TRIM(), using UPPER/LOWER/PROPER for text standardisation, and checking data against source documents.
topics.goal: Use Excel functions and proofreading techniques to verify, clean, and standardise data.

topics.title: Time Management & Productivity
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Work efficiently under deadlines. Covers prioritising tasks when multiple requests arrive, using calendar and reminder tools to manage deadlines, setting up a daily work checklist, understanding the difference between urgent and important tasks, and communicating delays professionally.
topics.goal: Apply time management techniques to prioritise data entry workloads and meet deadlines consistently.

topics.title: Batch Operations & Excel Macros
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Automate repetitive data entry tasks in Excel. Covers using Flash Fill to extract and reformat data automatically, using AutoFill for sequences and patterns, and recording a simple macro to repeat a multi-step formatting or data entry task with a single button press.
topics.goal: Use Flash Fill, AutoFill, and recorded macros to automate repetitive Excel data entry tasks.

topics.title: Document Management & Filing
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Organise documents professionally. Covers file naming conventions (date-first, version numbers, descriptive names), creating a logical folder hierarchy, archiving old documents, using search to locate files quickly, and understanding basic document retention and disposal policies in a workplace context.
topics.goal: Apply professional file naming conventions and folder structures to manage workplace documents efficiently.

topics.title: CSS Grid
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the CSS Grid container and item model, grid-template-columns and grid-template-rows, the fr unit, gap, grid-template-areas, auto-fill and auto-fit with minmax(), and the explicit vs implicit grid. Includes when to choose Grid over Flexbox and how they work together.
topics.goal: Build two-dimensional page structures and component layouts using CSS Grid, including named grid areas and responsive auto-fill grids.

topics.title: What is Cloud Computing?
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn what cloud computing means and how it differs from traditional on-premises infrastructure. Covers key benefits like elasticity, global scale, and on-demand resource provisioning.
topics.goal: Understand the core concept of cloud computing and why organisations use it.

topics.title: Service Models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Explore the three main cloud service models: Infrastructure-as-a-Service, Platform-as-a-Service, and Software-as-a-Service, including what responsibilities remain with the user in each model.
topics.goal: Explain the differences between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS and when to use each.

topics.title: Deployment Models: Public, Private, Hybrid
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the different deployment models used in cloud computing: public cloud, private cloud, hybrid cloud, and multi-cloud, and the trade-offs between them.
topics.goal: Choose the right cloud deployment model for a given business scenario.

topics.title: Cloud Networking Basics
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how cloud networks are built using virtual networks, subnets, routing, and IP addressing. Covers connectivity patterns like public endpoints, private networks, and VPC/VNet equivalents.
topics.goal: Describe how cloud networking enables secure application connectivity.

topics.title: Cloud Storage & Data Services
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Explore cloud storage options and data service categories. Covers object storage, block storage, file storage, managed databases, and when to choose each type.
topics.goal: Match cloud storage solutions to common application data needs.

topics.title: Identity, Access & Security
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Cover the fundamentals of cloud identity and access management, shared responsibility, securing resources, and protecting data in transit and at rest.
topics.goal: Apply provider-neutral security principles to cloud infrastructure.

topics.title: Cost Management & Optimization
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn cost management strategies for cloud environments, including resource sizing, auto-scaling, and rightsizing to avoid overspend while maintaining performance.
topics.goal: Identify ways to optimise cloud costs without sacrificing reliability.

topics.title: Cloud Compliance & Governance
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand cloud governance, compliance frameworks, and operational controls. Covers tagging, audit logging, data residency, and the role of policies in secure cloud operations.
topics.goal: Describe provider-agnostic governance practices for cloud platforms.

topics.title: Git Fundamentals
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the basic Git workflow: initializing repositories, staging changes, creating commits, and understanding the commit history.
topics.goal: Use Git to track changes and create a clean commit history.

topics.title: Branching & Merging
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand how branches work, why feature branches are useful, and how to merge changes safely using merge commits or rebasing.
topics.goal: Apply branching strategies to manage parallel work and avoid conflicts.

topics.title: Remote Collaboration
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Work with remote repositories using push, pull, fetch, and remotes. Covers tracking branches, pull requests, and resolving collaboration issues.
topics.goal: Collaborate with teammates using remote Git workflows.

topics.title: Merge Conflicts & Resolution
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn how merge conflicts occur and how to resolve them efficiently, including using Git tools and reviewing conflict markers.
topics.goal: Handle merge conflicts cleanly during branch integration.

topics.title: Tags, Releases & History
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn tagging, release versioning, and how to use Git history tools to inspect commits, bisect issues, and revert changes.
topics.goal: Manage releases and troubleshoot history with advanced Git tools.

topics.title: Git Workflows & Best Practices
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Explore common Git workflows such as Git Flow, trunk-based development, and GitHub Flow, and learn how to choose the right pattern for your team.
topics.goal: Select and apply an appropriate Git workflow for team delivery.

topics.title: Container Concepts
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the concept of containers, how they differ from virtual machines, and why containerisation matters for consistent application delivery.
topics.goal: Explain the purpose of containers and when to use them.

topics.title: Docker Images & Registries
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how Docker images are built, stored, and shared using registries. Covers image layers, tags, and pull/push workflows.
topics.goal: Create and publish Docker images for reuse and deployment.

topics.title: Dockerfile Authoring
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Author effective Dockerfiles using best practices, multi-stage builds, and caching strategies to build lean, secure container images.
topics.goal: Build optimized Docker images with reproducible Dockerfiles.

topics.title: Networking & Storage
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Understand Docker networking modes, container communication, and how to use volumes and bind mounts for persistent storage.
topics.goal: Configure container networking and persistent storage appropriately.

topics.title: Docker Compose
topics.label: Intermediate
