topics.description: Use Docker Compose to define and run multi-container applications, including service dependencies, environment variables, and volume mounts.
topics.goal: Build and run a multi-container application using Docker Compose.

topics.title: Docker Security & Best Practices
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn container security features and best practices, such as minimal base images, secrets management, image scanning, and runtime isolation.
topics.goal: Apply Docker security practices to hardened container deployments.

topics.title: Kubernetes Architecture
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the key Kubernetes components: control plane, nodes, pods, and how they work together to run containerised applications.
topics.goal: Describe the architecture of a Kubernetes cluster.

topics.title: Pods, Deployments & ReplicaSets
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand Kubernetes workload objects such as Pods, Deployments, and ReplicaSets, and how they enable scaling and self-healing.
topics.goal: Create and manage basic Kubernetes workloads.

topics.title: Services & Networking
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn how Kubernetes exposes applications using Services, DNS, and Ingress resources, including cluster networking and load balancing.
topics.goal: Connect Kubernetes workloads to users and other services securely.

topics.title: ConfigMaps & Secrets
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage configuration and sensitive data in Kubernetes using ConfigMaps and Secrets, and understand best practices for environment-specific settings.
topics.goal: Store and inject config and secrets into Kubernetes workloads safely.

topics.title: Storage & Volumes
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Explore Kubernetes storage options, including persistent volumes, persistent volume claims, and storage classes.
topics.goal: Use persistent storage in Kubernetes applications.

topics.title: Scaling, Rollouts & Updates
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn scaling and rollout techniques such as rolling updates, rollbacks, and horizontal pod autoscaling for resilient deployments.
topics.goal: Operate Kubernetes workloads through updates and scaling events.

topics.title: Observability & Troubleshooting
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand monitoring and logging practices for Kubernetes, including health checks, pod status, and troubleshooting common cluster issues.
topics.goal: Observe and troubleshoot Kubernetes applications in production.

topics.title: CI/CD Fundamentals
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn the goals of CI/CD and how automated build, test, and deployment processes improve software delivery quality and speed.
topics.goal: Explain the purpose of CI/CD in modern engineering teams.

topics.title: Build Automation
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand build automation, dependency management, and how code is compiled, packaged, or containerised as part of a CI pipeline.
topics.goal: Design an automated build stage for a delivery pipeline.

topics.title: Automated Testing
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn how unit, integration, and smoke tests fit into pipelines and why early feedback in CI is critical for quality.
topics.goal: Integrate automated tests into a CI/CD workflow.

topics.title: Deployment Strategies
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Explore deployment strategies such as blue-green, canary, and rolling updates to reduce risk during production releases.
topics.goal: Choose a safe release strategy for a deployment pipeline.

topics.title: Pipeline as Code
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn how to define build and deployment pipelines as code, version them with your application, and make pipeline changes reviewable.
topics.goal: Author and maintain pipeline definitions in source control.

topics.title: Release Observability
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand the importance of release observability, release gating, rollback triggers, and validating deployments in production.
topics.goal: Monitor and validate deployment outcomes for safer releases.

topics.title: DevOps Culture & Collaboration
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the DevOps philosophy: breaking silos between Dev and Ops, continuous feedback loops, shared ownership, blameless post-mortems, and the CALMS framework.
topics.goal: Describe the DevOps feedback loop and explain how shared ownership changes the way teams ship software.

topics.title: Infrastructure as Code
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Provision and manage infrastructure declaratively using tools like Terraform and CloudFormation. Understand idempotency, state management, and the difference between mutable and immutable infrastructure.
topics.goal: Write a Terraform script that provisions a cloud VM, apply it, and destroy the resources cleanly.

topics.title: Configuration Management
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Manage server configuration at scale with tools like Ansible: idempotent playbooks, inventory management, roles, and secrets handling.
topics.goal: Write an Ansible playbook that installs and configures a web server across multiple hosts.

topics.title: Observability & Monitoring
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Instrument applications and infrastructure with the three pillars of observability: logs, metrics, and traces. Use Prometheus, Grafana, and the ELK stack for real-time insight.
topics.goal: Set up Prometheus and Grafana to monitor a running service and create an alert for high error rates.

topics.title: Incident Response & Reliability
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Handle production incidents professionally: define SLOs and SLAs, run on-call rotations, conduct blameless post-mortems, and apply SRE practices to improve reliability over time.
topics.goal: Define SLOs for a service, write an incident runbook, and conduct a structured post-mortem review.

topics.title: Continuous Improvement
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Embed continuous improvement into the engineering culture: measure DORA metrics, run retrospectives, automate toil away, and drive measurable delivery performance improvements.
topics.goal: Calculate DORA metrics for your team's last quarter and propose one automation to reduce toil.

topics.title: Relational Data Model
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand the relational data model, including tables, rows, columns, primary keys, and foreign keys.
topics.goal: Explain how relational data is structured and related.

topics.title: SQL SELECT & Filtering
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Learn how to retrieve data using SELECT statements, apply filters with WHERE, and sort results with ORDER BY.
topics.goal: Query relational data using basic SQL statements.

topics.title: JOINs & Aggregation
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Use JOINs to combine multiple tables and aggregation functions with GROUP BY to summarise relational data.
topics.goal: Write SQL queries that join tables and produce summary results.

topics.title: Schema Design & Normalization
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Design relational schemas using normalization principles to reduce redundancy and improve data integrity.
topics.goal: Design a normalized relational schema for a simple application.

topics.title: Indexes & Performance
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn how indexes improve query performance, how to choose the right index types, and what trade-offs to expect.
topics.goal: Use indexes to optimise common relational queries.

topics.title: Transactions & Concurrency
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand transactions, ACID properties, isolation levels, and how relational databases handle concurrent updates.
topics.goal: Explain the role of transactions in reliable relational systems.

topics.title: Jenkins Fundamentals
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Introduction to Jenkins, its role in CI/CD, core components (master, agents), and common use cases for build and release automation.
topics.goal: Explain Jenkins architecture and create a basic job to automate a build.

topics.title: Pipeline as Code (Jenkinsfile)
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Author declarative and scripted Jenkins pipelines using Jenkinsfile, stages, steps, and environment handling to model end-to-end delivery flows.
topics.goal: Create and version a Jenkinsfile that builds, tests, and packages an application.

topics.title: Agents, Executors and Scaling
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Understand how Jenkins agents and executors work, provisioning dynamic agents (Docker, Kubernetes), and strategies to scale builds safely.
topics.goal: Configure Jenkins agents and design a scalable build runner strategy.

topics.title: Integrating with SCM and Build Tools
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Connect Jenkins to source control (Git), trigger builds via webhooks, and integrate with build tools and artifact repositories for end-to-end workflows.
topics.goal: Wire up SCM triggers and artifact publishing in a Jenkins pipeline.

topics.title: Security, Credentials and Secrets
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Manage credentials securely in Jenkins, apply least-privilege for build agents, secure pipeline inputs, and harden Jenkins infrastructure.
topics.goal: Implement secure credential management and access controls for Jenkins pipelines.

topics.title: Shared Libraries and Reusability
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Create and consume Jenkins Shared Libraries to centralize common pipeline logic, tests, and deployment routines for maintainable pipelines.
topics.goal: Author a reusable shared library and refactor pipelines to use it.

topics.title: NoSQL & MongoDB Essentials
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Understand NoSQL concepts and MongoDB fundamentals: documents, collections, BSON, and when to choose document databases.
topics.goal: Explain MongoDB's document model and use it for simple data storage needs.

topics.title: Data Modeling in MongoDB
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn embedding vs referencing, schema design patterns, modelling one-to-many relationships, and trade-offs for read/write patterns.
topics.goal: Design an efficient document model for a sample application.

topics.title: CRUD and Aggregation Framework
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Perform CRUD operations, and use the aggregation framework for transforms, grouping, and analytics within the database.
topics.goal: Write aggregation pipelines to reshape and summarise data.

topics.title: Indexing and Query Performance
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Understand indexes, index types, explain plans, and strategies to improve read performance while balancing write costs.
topics.goal: Add appropriate indexes and interpret explain output to optimise queries.

topics.title: Replication and High Availability
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Configure replica sets for redundancy, understand election behavior, read preferences, and how to operate a highly available cluster.
topics.goal: Design a replica set for fault tolerance and failover.

topics.title: Sharding and Scalability
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Learn sharding fundamentals, choosing shard keys, balancing, and considerations for large-scale MongoDB deployments.
topics.goal: Plan a sharded deployment for heavy-write workloads.

topics.title: Backups, Security and Operations
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Perform backups and restores, secure MongoDB (authentication, authorization, encryption), and monitor health and resource usage.
topics.goal: Implement backup and security best-practices for a MongoDB deployment.

topics.title: Redux Pattern & NgRX Overview
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers the problem that global state management solves, the Flux/Redux mental model (single source of truth, state is read-only, changes through pure functions), how NgRX implements Redux for Angular, and a tour of the NgRX package ecosystem: @ngrx/store, @ngrx/effects, @ngrx/entity, @ngrx/router-store, @ngrx/component-store, and @ngrx/signals.
topics.goal: Explain why unidirectional data flow matters, describe the Redux cycle (action → reducer → store → selector → view), and identify which NgRX package solves which problem.

topics.title: NgRX Store & DevTools
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers bootstrapping the NgRX store with provideStore() and provideState() in a standalone Angular application, defining feature state slices, registering the StoreDevtools module for time-travel debugging, inspecting dispatched actions and state snapshots in the Redux DevTools browser extension, and understanding the global state tree.
topics.goal: Bootstrap a working NgRX store in an Angular application, register feature states, and use Redux DevTools to inspect and replay state changes.

topics.title: Actions
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers creating typed actions with createAction() and props<T>(), grouping related actions with createActionGroup() to reduce boilerplate, NgRX action naming conventions (describing events rather than commands), dispatching actions from components and services via Store.dispatch(), and understanding actions as the single communication channel into the store.
topics.goal: Define strongly-typed actions using createAction and createActionGroup, follow naming conventions, and dispatch actions from Angular components and services.

topics.title: Reducers & State
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers defining initial state with TypeScript interfaces, writing pure reducer functions with createReducer() and on(), enforcing immutability (spread operator and structured clone patterns), understanding how reducers are composed into the global state tree, and registering feature reducers with provideState().
topics.goal: Write pure, immutable reducers with createReducer that correctly handle multiple actions and compose into the global NgRX state tree.

topics.title: Selectors
topics.label: Foundation
topics.description: Covers createFeatureSelector() to access feature state slices, composing selectors with createSelector(), memoization and why it prevents unnecessary re-renders, projector functions for derived data, selecting state in components with store.select() and the modern store.selectSignal() for Angular Signals integration, and selector testing strategies.
topics.goal: Build a selector hierarchy using createSelector and createFeatureSelector, understand memoization, and connect selectors to Angular components using both observables and selectSignal().

topics.title: NgRX Effects
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers side-effect management with createEffect(), the Actions$ stream and ofType() operator, choosing the right flattening operator (switchMap for cancellable, concatMap for sequential, exhaustMap for ignoring concurrent, mergeMap for parallel), handling errors with catchError and returning failure actions, dispatch: false for non-dispatching effects, and testing effects with provideMockActions.
topics.goal: Handle HTTP calls and other async side effects with NgRX Effects, choose the correct RxJS flattening operator for the use case, and handle errors gracefully without crashing the effect stream.

topics.title: NgRX Entity
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers the motivation for @ngrx/entity (normalised collection state), creating an EntityAdapter with createEntityAdapter(), the EntityState shape (ids array + entities dictionary), built-in CRUD adapter methods (addOne, addMany, setAll, updateOne, upsertOne, removeOne, removeMany), and the pre-built selector factory (selectAll, selectEntities, selectIds, selectTotal). Includes a brief introduction to @ngrx/data for high-level CRUD abstraction.
topics.goal: Manage normalised entity collections in NgRX using EntityAdapter, reduce reducer boilerplate for CRUD operations, and query entities efficiently with the generated selectors.

topics.title: NgRX Router Store
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Covers connecting Angular Router state to the NgRX store with provideRouterStore() and routerReducer, using the built-in router selectors (selectRouteParam, selectQueryParam, selectUrl, selectRouteData), triggering navigation from NgRX effects using Angular Router, serialising minimal router state for performance, and reading route information from the store rather than directly injecting ActivatedRoute.
topics.goal: Integrate Angular Router state into the NgRX store, query URL parameters and route data via selectors, and trigger programmatic navigation from effects.

topics.title: Component Store
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers @ngrx/component-store as a solution for local component-level state (avoiding global store pollution), defining state with ComponentStore<T>, mutating state with updater() and patchState(), reading state reactively with select() and the vm$ ViewModel pattern, handling async operations with the built-in effect(), and deciding when to use ComponentStore versus the global store versus plain Angular Signals.
topics.goal: Manage component-scoped state with ComponentStore, expose a clean ViewModel observable to templates, and choose appropriately between local and global state solutions.

topics.title: NgRX Signals Store
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Covers @ngrx/signals as the modern signal-native state primitive, creating a SignalStore with signalStore(), composing store features with withState(), withComputed(), withMethods(), withEntities(), and withHooks(), bridging RxJS with signals using rxMethod(), updating state atomically with patchState(), building custom store features for reuse, and understanding how NgRX Signals relates to and differs from the classic @ngrx/store.
topics.goal: Build reactive, signal-based state stores with @ngrx/signals, compose reusable store features, and integrate RxJS-based async logic via rxMethod().

topics.title: Angular Modules
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Understand how NgModule organises an Angular app into cohesive blocks — covering declarations, imports, exports, providers, and the difference between feature and shared modules.
topics.goal: Describe the role of NgModule, set up feature modules, create shared modules, and understand how Angular resolves components and services at the module level.

topics.title: Custom Pipes
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Go beyond Angular's built-in pipes by creating reusable, parameterised transformation pipes using the @Pipe decorator and PipeTransform interface.
topics.goal: Create pure and impure custom pipes, pass parameters via the pipe syntax, declare them in modules, and understand performance implications.

topics.title: Lazy Loading
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Split your app into feature modules that load only when needed, dramatically reducing initial bundle size and improving startup performance.
topics.goal: Configure lazy-loaded routes with loadChildren, apply PreloadAllModules and custom preloading strategies, and verify bundle splits in the build output.

topics.title: Advanced Change Detection
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Master Angular's change detection engine: the difference between Default and OnPush strategies, Zone.js internals, manual triggering, and detach/reattach patterns.
topics.goal: Apply ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush correctly, use ChangeDetectorRef to trigger or skip checks, and run tasks outside NgZone for performance gains.

topics.title: Component State Patterns
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Explore patterns for managing local and shared component state: smart vs presentational components, BehaviorSubject services, and when to reach for a state library.
topics.goal: Choose the right state strategy for a given feature, implement a reactive shared-state service with BehaviorSubject, and avoid common anti-patterns like nested subscriptions.

topics.title: NgRx
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Manage application state predictably with NgRx: Store, Actions, Reducers, Selectors, and Effects for complex, testable data flows.
topics.goal: Implement a feature slice with NgRx, dispatch typed actions, derive state with memoized selectors, and handle API side effects with Effects.

topics.title: Angular Signals
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Explore Angular's built-in signal primitives (Angular 17+): signal(), computed(), and effect(), enabling fine-grained reactivity without Zone.js overhead.
topics.goal: Replace BehaviorSubjects with signals where appropriate, derive computed values, react to changes with effects, and understand the signal-based change detection model.

topics.title: Advanced RxJS
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Move beyond basic map and catchError: multicasting, higher-order observables, backpressure, custom operators, and patterns for complex async workflows in Angular.
topics.goal: Use switchMap, concatMap, mergeMap, and exhaustMap correctly, implement multicasting with shareReplay, write custom RxJS operators, and avoid common memory-leak pitfalls.

topics.title: Performance Optimization
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Identify and fix Angular performance bottlenecks: bundle analysis, tree-shaking, defer blocks, virtual scrolling, trackBy, and image optimisation.
topics.goal: Profile an Angular app with Chrome DevTools, reduce bundle size with source-map-explorer, apply @defer for heavy components, and implement virtual scrolling for large lists.

topics.title: Core Web Vitals
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand and optimise the three Google Core Web Vitals — Largest Contentful Paint, Cumulative Layout Shift, and Interaction to Next Paint — in the context of Angular SPAs.
topics.goal: Measure LCP, CLS, and INP using Lighthouse and web-vitals.js, identify Angular-specific causes of each metric degrading, and apply targeted fixes.

topics.title: Server-Side Rendering
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Render Angular applications on the server with Angular Universal (now built into Angular 17+ via @angular/ssr) for improved SEO, First Contentful Paint, and Time to Interactive.
topics.goal: Add SSR to an existing Angular project, handle browser-only APIs safely, implement hydration, and deploy a Node.js SSR server.

topics.title: Angular Security
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Understand Angular's built-in XSS protections, use DomSanitizer correctly, implement CSRF mitigation, attach JWT tokens via HTTP interceptors, and follow OWASP guidelines.
topics.goal: Identify common Angular security pitfalls, write a JWT auth interceptor, sanitize dynamic HTML safely, and configure Content Security Policy headers.

topics.title: Testing
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Write reliable unit and integration tests for Angular components, services, and pipes using Jasmine, Karma, and Angular TestBed, with mocked HTTP via HttpClientTestingModule.
topics.goal: Test a component with TestBed, mock services with spies, verify template output, test reactive forms, and simulate HTTP requests without hitting real APIs.

topics.title: Dynamic Components
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Create and insert Angular components programmatically at runtime using ViewContainerRef, ComponentRef, and the new @defer and @if control flow blocks.
topics.goal: Dynamically render a component into a container, pass inputs at runtime, destroy components cleanly, and build a simple modal or tooltip system.

topics.title: Standalone Components
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Learn Angular's modern module-free API: bootstrapApplication(), standalone: true, provideRouter(), importProvidersFrom(), and the standalone component mental model.
topics.goal: Migrate a module-based feature to standalone, bootstrap an app without AppModule, and understand how DI works without NgModule boundaries.

topics.title: Error Handling
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Build resilient Angular apps: global ErrorHandler, HTTP error interceptors, retry strategies with RxJS, user-facing error states, and structured error boundaries.
topics.goal: Implement a global ErrorHandler, write an HTTP interceptor that retries on 5xx errors, and present meaningful feedback to users on failures.

topics.title: Content Projection
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Compose reusable component APIs using ng-content, multi-slot projection with select, ContentChild, ContentChildren, and ng-template with TemplateRef.
topics.goal: Build a card component with named slots, project conditional content, and query projected children with ContentChild.

topics.title: Custom Directives
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Extend HTML with custom behaviour: build attribute directives with ElementRef, Renderer2, @HostListener, @HostBinding, and write simple structural directives with TemplateRef and ViewContainerRef.
topics.goal: Create an attribute directive that changes element style on hover, and a structural directive that conditionally renders content with context data.

topics.title: Advanced Forms
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Go beyond basic reactive forms: ControlValueAccessor for custom form controls, FormArray for dynamic fields, cross-field validators, nested FormGroups, and typed reactive forms.
topics.goal: Implement a custom form control with ControlValueAccessor, build a dynamic field list with FormArray, and add a cross-field validator to a FormGroup.

topics.title: Advanced Routing
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Master Angular router's advanced capabilities: route resolvers for pre-fetching data, named/secondary router outlets, route parameters and query params strategies, and functional guards.
topics.goal: Write a route resolver that fetches data before navigation, implement named outlets for a side-panel layout, and convert class-based guards to functional guards.

topics.title: Angular Animations
topics.label: Intermediate
topics.description: Add motion to Angular apps with @angular/animations: triggers, states, transitions, keyframes, animateChild, and query/stagger for list animations.
topics.goal: Animate a route transition, build an expanding panel with state-based animation, and stagger a list of items on entry.

topics.title: Angular CDK
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Use Angular CDK to build robust UI primitives: Overlay for popups/tooltips, Portal for dynamic content, DragDrop for sortable lists, FocusTrap for accessibility, and virtual scrolling.
topics.goal: Build a custom tooltip using CDK Overlay, implement drag-and-drop reordering with CdkDragDrop, and trap focus inside a modal with FocusTrap.

topics.title: PWA & Service Workers
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Convert an Angular app into a Progressive Web App using @angular/service-worker: caching strategies, offline support, push notifications, app update flows, and Web App Manifest.
topics.goal: Add service worker support to an Angular app, configure asset and data caching strategies in ngsw-config.json, and prompt users to reload when a new version deploys.

topics.title: i18n
topics.label: Advanced
topics.description: Internationalise Angular apps using built-in i18n with $localize, the Angular CLI extract-i18n workflow, locale-aware pipes (date, currency, number), and ngx-translate for runtime language switching.
topics.goal: Mark strings for translation, extract message files with ng extract-i18n, build locale-specific bundles, and implement runtime language switching with ngx-translate.
